---
title: "STSCI 5740 Project Report"
format: pdf
fontsize: 12pt
papersize: a4
linestretch: 1.5
---

## Introduction

This project examines hospital readmission patterns among patients with diabetes, with emphasis on broad trends across patient groups. The analysis uses the Diabetes 130-US hospitals dataset (1999-2008) from the UCI Machine Learning Repository. Project materials and updates are available on GitHub: [stsci5740-project](https://github.com/kelvinchi02/stsci5740-project).

## Data Cleaning

This section documents the data cleaning workflow applied in `STSCI5740_Data.ipynb`. The purpose is to construct an analysis-ready patient-level table while preserving clinical interpretability.

### 1. Patient-Level Deduplication

To enforce independent observations, only the first encounter for each unique `patient_nbr` was retained.

- Rows before deduplication: 101,766
- Rows after deduplication: 71,518

### 2. Sparse Feature Removal

Columns with substantial missingness were removed:

- `weight`
- `medical_specialty`
- `payer_code`

After column removal, the dataset dimension was 71,518 x 47.

### 3. Missing and Invalid Value Treatment

Missing and invalid values were handled as follows:

- `race`: missing values were mapped to `Other` (1,948 values filled after deduplication).
- `max_glu_serum` and `A1Cresult`: `None` was preserved as an explicit category representing tests not ordered.
- `gender`: rows labeled `Unknown/Invalid` were dropped (3 rows).
- `diag_1`: rows with missing primary diagnosis were dropped (11 rows).
- `diag_2` and `diag_3`: missing values were mapped to `None` and treated as a meaningful category.

### 4. Diagnosis Code Consolidation

`diag_1`, `diag_2`, and `diag_3` ICD-9 diagnosis codes were consolidated into nine clinical groups:

- Circulatory
- Respiratory
- Digestive
- Diabetes
- Injury
- Musculoskeletal
- Genitourinary
- Neoplasms
- Other

### 5. Metadata Label Mapping

Administrative ID fields were converted from integer codes to descriptive labels using the parsed IDS mapping tables:

- `admission_type_id`
- `discharge_disposition_id`
- `admission_source_id`

### 6. Final Cleaning Output

After row-level cleaning and diagnosis recoding, the cleaned patient-level dataset contained 71,504 records.
